title: Hymns to the Virgin and Christ, the Parliament of Devils, and Other Religious Poems (Furnivall twenty-six-item selection)
summary: The pinned repository XML copies U-M CME item ANT9911.0001.001, an uncorrected-OCR encoding of the 1895 reprint title leaf and printed pp. 1–128 from Furnivall’s twenty-six-item 1867 selection. The 1895 reprint is modeled separately from the 1867 edition; the edition transcribes Lambeth MS 853 and one Vernon stream, while Wright’s three pre-1867 transcripts mediate the final Trinity sources. MS 583 remains an unresolved historical citation, separate from MS 853. The graph contains 24 entities, 31 relations, 27 access records, 28 rights records, 29 evidence records, and 9 open questions; 42 access-linked local copies sit within a 47-file fixity-verified cache projection.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME encoding ANT9911.0001.001
entities.description: The 2006 U-M item derived by uncorrected OCR from the 1895 reprint and encoding its title leaf plus printed pp. 1–128.
notes: The item header says uncorrected OCR and no content editing; the collection page describes later batches as manually keyed and proofread. The conflict remains unresolved.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 853

entities.label: Wright transcript of Trinity B.11.24 before 1867

entities.label: Wright transcript of Trinity O.9.38 before 1867

entities.label: Wright transcript of Trinity R.4.20 before 1867

editorial_practices.source_feature: Religious poems transmitted in several manuscript witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: Twenty-six-item edition-defined selection, with Kepe Wel printed in two parallel versions
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall selected and arranged three Virgin hymns, eight Christ-centered items, and fifteen other religious items; the manifest preserves that bounded editorial unit without treating it as one medieval work.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete 1895 print carrier with front and back matter
editorial_practices.representation: Encoded title leaf and printed pp. 1–128; other front matter and pp. 129–136 omitted
editorial_practices.description: The U-M encoding preserves the Furnivall selected body but omits the Halle extract, glossary, index, and non-title front matter.
editorial_practices.scope: ANT9911.0001.001 title leaf and XML nodes 3–19

editorial_practices.source_feature: Interleaved parallel Vernon and Lambeth print layout on pp. 106–113
editorial_practices.representation: Consecutive analytical XML version streams 15.1 and 15.2
editorial_practices.description: The encoding converts Furnivall’s page-by-page parallel Kepe Wel layout into consecutive version divisions. Those divisions represent two witness streams of one intellectual-work family; the serialization does not create two intellectual works.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two complete witness versions of Kepe Wel Cristes Comaundement
editorial_practices.representation: Interleaved parallel Vernon and Lambeth streams on printed pp. 106–113
editorial_practices.description: The edition represents both complete witness streams in parallel. The metadata counts one intellectual-work family while retaining both streams analytically.

evidence.summary: The exact current catalog supplies shelfmark, date/physical description, item loci, The Belief title and incipit, MS 853 p. 54 match, microfilm references, and publication history.

evidence.citation: Complete current Lambeth LUNA MS853-star search response
evidence.summary: The cached query returns 15 records: 13 manuscript-image objects and two unrelated 1937 print records. It proves the current public selection inventory, not absence of every mediated surrogate.

evidence.citation: UK Intellectual Property Office copyright-duration guidance
evidence.summary: Official guidance supports the edition-term analysis and preserves the potential pre-1989 unpublished-work transition through 2039 for Wright transcripts.

evidence.citation: Direct catalog synthesis of pinned XML, both Furnivall editions, exact manuscript canvases, DIMEV releases, and cache audit
evidence.summary: Direct visual and computational inspection establishes the bounded title-leaf/pp. 1–128 selection, 33-part count hierarchy, 26 count-bearing works, 27 represented streams, preferred Belief title, 3,360/392 count-once extent, 3,464/405 representation extent, 24/31/27/28/29/9 lineage counts, and 47-file cache projection with 42 attached access copies.
evidence.locator: XML title/body; edition title/preface/pp. 1–128; exact manuscript target canvases; all manifest arrays
notes: No ignored build packet path is cited as evidence.

open_questions.question: Is Furnivall’s MS 583 citation a transposition for MS 853?
next_steps: Keep the entities separate and relation:08 tentative.
Seek Lambeth historical shelfmark evidence before asserting identity.

open_questions.question: Do MS Film 1232 or World Microfilms Section I Reel 15 cover all of MS 853, and can one be supplied or digitized?
next_steps: Verify microfilm inventory and completeness before proposing new photography.

open_questions.question: Do the three Wright working transcripts survive, and where are they held?
next_steps: After authorization, ask EETS, Trinity, or relevant archives; do not infer destruction from present non-discovery.

open_questions.question: Were Wright’s transcripts ever published, and does UK transitional protection through 2039 apply?
next_steps: Establish survival and publication history before any reuse conclusion.

open_questions.question: What rights did the CME repository affirmer hold and intend to waive in inherited U-M/XML/edition content?
next_steps: Keep the software-worded Unlicense scope narrow pending provenance evidence.

open_questions.question: How does the item’s OCR/no-correction header relate to the collection page’s manual-keying/proofreading description?
next_steps: Preserve both statements and ask U-M for item-level production history.

open_questions.question: Is a complete public package of the 164 linked TIFFs stated in the U-M header available?
next_steps: Use ordinary public routes only; do not bypass the scripted-route 403 response.

open_questions.question: Will U-M/CME correct XML ‘The Relief’ to printed and cataloged ‘The Belief’?
next_steps: Retain Relief as an encoded OCR variant and Belief as the preferred part title regardless of upstream correction.

open_questions.question: Are the five Latin lines before The Belief a separately circulating work?
next_steps: Keep them as null-count analytical paratext unless exact work-identity evidence establishes otherwise.

review_notes: The four complete public manuscript IIIF bundles cover every provider-exposed canvas at the recorded request profile; only target work portions are cataloged.
The thirteen Lambeth objects are complete one-canvas provider objects but partial coverage of MS 853.
No direct edition-to-Trinity witness relation is asserted because Furnivall explicitly identifies Wright transcript intermediaries.
Every rights record joins an access record for the same entity; modern images, provider terms, edition text, and repository-license scope remain separate layers.
